stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Generally, a metal will expand when heated.
  • The expansion of a material due to heating is known as thermal expansion.
  • A 2018 study led by Hiroshi Tanaka and Lourdes García tested the thermal expansion of various rods.
  • When a 200-millimeter-long sample of copper rod was heated by 100°C, its length increased by 0.34 millimeters.
  • When a 250-millimeter-long sample of aluminum rod was heated by 100°C, its length increased by 0.58 millimeters.
stem: The student wants to contrast the two samples. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: When the rods were heated as part of the 2018 study, the length of both samples increased.
In 2018, researchers studied the effect of thermal expansion on various rods, including samples of copper and aluminum.
The aluminum rod sample used in the 2018 study was longer than the copper rod sample.
Heating a metal will generally cause its length to increase, a process known as thermal expansion.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The sentence contrasts the two samples, noting that the aluminum rod sample, at 250 millimeters long, was longer than the copper rod sample, which was 200 millimeters long.

Choice A is incorrect because the sentence mentions a similarity between the copper and aluminum samples; it does not contrast the two samples. Choice B is incorrect because the sentence indicates that both samples were part of the 2018 study; it does not contrast the two samples. Choice D is incorrect because the sentence describes the process of thermal expansion; it does not contrast the two samples.
